equity.title: Pay Equity Analysis
equity.no_profiles: Save at least 2 profiles to see the equity analysis.
equity.ratio_warn: Equity ratio exceeds 2×. Management 3.0 recommends keeping this ratio low for transparent compensation.
equity.ratio_high: Equity ratio exceeds 3×. Consider reviewing factor values for significant pay disparities.

builder.title: Formula parameters
builder.subtitle: Adjust min, max, and step for each factor. Sliders on the calculator screen stay within these bounds.
builder.disclaimer: Changing ranges does not alter saved profiles — only how new adjustments behave in the calculator.
builder.template_hint: Templates set a starting configuration. All values can be freely edited after applying.

balanced.name: Balanced
balanced.desc: Equal factor weights — a good starting point for teams new to transparent compensation formulas.

remote_first.name: Remote-first team
remote_first.desc: Higher location coefficient for hub cities. Moderate seniority and baseline performance range.

startup.name: Startup (growth-focused)
startup.desc: Lower base, strong skills and performance emphasis. Reflects equity-supplemented compensation.

enterprise.name: Enterprise flat band
enterprise.desc: Narrow multiplier range (0.9–1.1×) for all factors. Predictable, compliance-friendly structure.

home.headline: Transparent Salary Formula
home.subheadline: Make compensation fair, visible, and formula-driven — not opaque negotiation.
home.why_body: When salary rules are hidden, people fill the gap with rumors and assumptions. Management 3.0 advocates for making the formula public — not the individual numbers — so everyone understands how compensation is determined and can see it is fair.
home.step1: Set your market base salary for the role.
home.step2: Adjust multipliers: seniority, skills, location, performance.
home.step3: See the formula compute the result in real time.
home.step4: Compare profiles or export for your records.

calculator.title: Salary Calculator
calculator.wp_no_data: No Work Profiles found. Open the Work Profiles app first.

comparison.title: Profile Comparison
comparison.empty: Add at least one saved profile to compare.
comparison.team_rate_shared: Your team hourly rate has been shared with Scrum Facilitator.

scenario.title: Scenario Comparison
scenario.name_placeholder: Scenario name (e.g. Latin America rates, 2025 revision)
scenario.save_hint: Saves the current formula parameters (min, max, step, value) as a named scenario.
scenario.empty_hint: Open the Formula tab, adjust parameters, and save a scenario.
scenario.diff_label: Delta values are shown relative to the first (baseline) scenario.
scenario.log_change: Log this change in Change Planner
scenario.change_logged: Change logged. Open Change Planner to complete the record.

learn.title: About the Salary Formula
learn.intro: The Management 3.0 Salary Formula is a structured approach to transparent compensation. Instead of arbitrary negotiation, every salary is the product of clearly defined factors that anyone can inspect.
learn.p1_body: Publish the formula, not necessarily every individual's number. People should be able to compute what their salary would be if they move to a different level.
learn.p2_body: Salaries should not depend on how well someone negotiates. The formula removes that bias and makes outcomes consistent.
learn.p3_body: Review the formula periodically (e.g. annually) with team input. The formula itself should evolve.
learn.pitfall1: Overcomplicating — too many factors make the formula opaque again.
learn.pitfall2: Ignoring market data — the base rate must reflect real market rates.
learn.pitfall3: Static formulas — if the formula never changes, it stops reflecting reality.
learn.pitfall4: Gaming — be explicit about how each factor is assessed (not self-rated).

base.label: Base Market Rate
base.desc: The annual market salary for this role and function in this region, before any multipliers. Source: job boards, salary surveys, HR data.

seniority.label: Seniority Multiplier
seniority.desc: Reflects experience level and scope of impact. From intern (0.6×) to C-level (1.8×). Defined by agreed career levels in your org.

skills.label: Skills Score
skills.desc: Based on a structured skill assessment (not self-rating). Measures breadth and depth of relevant competencies: 0.7× (narrow/developing) to 1.4× (broad/expert).

location.label: Location Coefficient
location.desc: Adjusts for cost of living differences between cities or countries. 1.0 = base city. Lower for lower-cost regions, higher for expensive metros.

performance.label: Performance Multiplier
performance.desc: Reflects team and individual performance over the review period. Typically assessed quarterly or annually. Range: 0.85× to 1.25×.
